notes:
chẳng means not at all or simply not, but with a stronger, more emotional tone than không. It often shows emphasis, annoyance, or certainty.

It goes before verbs and adjectives to negate them. It feels a bit more expressive or dramatic than neutral không.

Very common in fixed phrases like chẳng ai (nobody) and chẳng bao giờ (never).  
Close synonym: không (more neutral); casual spoken form: chả.
exampleSentences: Anh chẳng muốn đi xem phim tối nay vì mệt quá.
Em chẳng hiểu bài toán này dù đã cố gắng nhiều lần.
Chị ấy nói rằng chị chẳng thích ăn đồ ngọt.

definitions.vi: Dùng để chỉ sự phủ định hoặc sự vắng mặt.
definitions.es: Se utiliza para indicar negación o ausencia.
definitions.en: Used to indicate negation or absence.

exampleSentencesTranslated: I do not at all want to go to the movies tonight  because I am too tired.
I do not at all understand this math problem even though I have tried many times.
She said that she does not at all like eating sweets.
notes_md:
**chẳng** means *not at all* or simply *not*, but with a stronger, more emotional tone than **không**. It often shows emphasis, annoyance, or certainty.

It goes before verbs and adjectives to negate them. It feels a bit more expressive or dramatic than neutral **không**.


Very common in fixed phrases like **chẳng ai** (nobody) and **chẳng bao giờ** (never).  
Close synonym: **không** (more neutral); casual spoken form: **chả**.

translation.en: I do not see this issue.
